summary: Tested iteration-2 features: per-phone OTP rate-limit, cancel & reschedule booking flows. 11/11 new pytest backend tests passed (rate-limit-2/2, cancel-4/4, reschedule-4/4, tracking-frozen-1/1). Frontend Playwright e2e covered: manage-section visibility per status, reschedule dialog defaults (24h ahead, today minDate), reschedule submit, cancel dialog 'Other' reason → custom textarea, red Cancelled banner with reason, tracking distance frozen on cancelled booking. All flows green.

issues: Booking summary shows 'Amount paid: ₹299' even when payment was never made (uses booking.price unconditionally). Should hide or relabel for unpaid/cancelled bookings.
Stat card 'Status: cancelled' — prettyStatus() has no mapping for 'cancelled', so it renders lowercase. Map it to 'Cancelled' for consistency.
Status timeline shows no greyed/strike-through state when status='cancelled'. Currently no timeline node lights up; consider showing a 'Cancelled' end-state.

action_items: (Optional polish) Fix 'Amount paid' label to reflect actual payment state on Tracking page
(Optional polish) Add 'cancelled' to prettyStatus() map for proper capitalisation
(Optional polish) Render terminal 'Cancelled' state on the progress timeline
critical_code_review_comments: Rate limit relies solely on db.otps records; if the OTP TTL index later drops rows < 30s old (it won't at 300s), rate-limit window correctness depends on TTL ≥ window. Currently OK (TTL 300s vs cooldown 30s).

_check_otp_rate_limit raises with the most-recent record before checking total count; if a phone hits MAX in window, it'll get 429 with cooldown msg first, then count-msg only after cooldown. Acceptable.
reschedule_booking always sets status='confirmed' if previous was paid/confirmed even though no new provider re-assignment is performed in code (provider object kept). Cosmetic only — no bug.

tracking endpoint advances provider position and may auto-flip status to 'on-the-way' very quickly (distance threshold 5km, start ~2.4km), so reschedule UI disappears within first poll on a paid booking. Matches product spec but means users have a tiny window post-payment to reschedule. Consider deferring auto-advance until N seconds after confirmed.

Frontend default reschedule slot is 24h after current scheduled_for, not 24h from now. If a user just booked for tomorrow and then reschedules, default jumps to day-after-tomorrow — fine, but min date is set to today (could let user pick a slot < lead-time and rely on backend 400). Already showing client-side error in submit handler. Good.
Tracking page's 'Amount paid' field is misleading on unpaid bookings; gate on booking.payment != null.
test_credentials: Phone: any random 10-digit (e.g. 9777xxxxxx); OTP visible at data-testid=demo-otp-code on /login.
seed_data_creation: Tests create per-run users/bookings with random phones starting 9. Bookings prefixed via TEST_ user names. No teardown (mock data, OTP TTL handles cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-2 suite is /app/backend/tests/test_rate_limit_cancel_reschedule.py (pytest, 11 tests). Iteration-1 suite still at /app/backend/tests/test_auth_bookings.py. To FE-test reschedule on a paid booking, you must act within 1 polling cycle (~3s) before tracking auto-advances status to on-the-way, OR test on a pending (unpaid) booking by creating it via API + navigating to /track/:id. Cancel works in any of {pending, paid, confirmed, on-the-way}. CancelDialog.jsx exposes data-testid='cancel-reason-other' (kebab) etc. RescheduleDialog default slot is 24h after the booking's existing scheduled_for, not now+24h.
